zh.Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔
zh.lifeSummary: 當William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry於大約1158年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾時，他的父親William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry54歲，母親Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun27歲。他和Isabel de Say至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1210年4月在英國素可阿盧德爾享年53歲。

zh-hans.Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry于大约1158年出生在英国英格兰賀福爾时，他的父亲William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry54岁，母亲Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun27岁。他和Isabel de Say至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1210年4月在英国素可阿盧德爾享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry was born about 1158, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry, was 54 and his mother, Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun, was 27. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Isabel de Say. He died in April 1210, in Arundel, Sussex, England, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry est né environ 1158, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Isabel de Say. Il est décédé en avril 1210, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry um 1158 in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry 54 und seine Mutter Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun 27. Er hatte mit Isabel de Say mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb im April 1210 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Arundel, Sussex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry è nato il circa 1158 nel Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry, aveva 54 anni e il suo madre, Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Isabel de Say. Morì nel aprile 1210, nel Arundel, Sussex, England, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestryが約1158年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestryは54歳，母Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clunは27歳であった。彼はIsabel de Sayとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1210年4月にイギリススセックスアルンデルで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry(이)가 대략 1158년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Isabel de Say(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1210년 4월 영국 수세스 아런들에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry nasceu aproximadamente 1158, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Isabel de Say. Ele faleceu em abril de 1210, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry родился около 1158 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun, 27. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Isabel de Say. Он умер апрель 1210 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William FitzAlan II 1st Lord of Owestry nació aproximadamente 1158, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Isabella de Saye, Baroness of Clun, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Isabel de Say. Murió en abril de 1210, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años.
